statement: The next improvement in V3 analysis quality will come more from adding table-first mining outputs (q-values, effect contributions, verdict-geometry certainty, bundle-policy deltas) than from adding more raw plots.
rationale: The current pipeline already has matched-delta inference and candidate findings, but exploration is still bottlenecked by manually browsing a large figure inventory.
keyAssumptions: A reproducible ranking of top samples, bundles, and contrasts is more useful than a broad unranked chart inventory.
validationCriteria: A new analysis pass can identify the top findings and top spot-check cases from tables before looking at any figures.
Most new report figures are rendered from ranked tables rather than discovered manually.
critiquePoints: Some visual structure may still be easier to detect in plots than in tables.
questions: What is the minimum canonical table set needed before any new plotting pass?
